title: 続きを/voteで続けて。 • consensus mode の前提を確認します。まず Claude と GLM の live lane を起動できるかを確認し、同時に現在の auth- stale 導線設計を維持したまま continuation 可能かを見ます。 • Ran which glm └ glm not found • Explored └ Read agent-glm-general-reviewer.json, agent-claude-general-reviewer.json ────────────────────────────────────────────────...
summary_text: - **Strong pass:** first-login recovery path is structurally solid (`/happy/auth/` + `returnTo` restore + auth-stale handling), so mobile re-entry continuity is credible. - **Strong pass:** mobile shell does provide bounded operational surfaces (`Home/Work/Live/Recover`) with safe recovery actions, which matches the “no destructive mobile control” intent. - **Failure mode:** first-login clarity is still status-strip driven, not truly first-class IA; auth state can feel secondary instead of becoming the explicit primary route decision screen. - **Failure mode:** task topology visibility is shallow (counts + top cards + route tags) and does not expose lane-level structure/operator context well enough for a Kernel control surface claim. - **Failure mode:** task-bound natural-language control is missing; commands are global composer-only, with no scoped “instruct this task/lane” action from task detail. - **False-complete risk:** cost/comms telemetry logic exists in domain/tests (`happy-control-surface`) but is not wired into rendered UI/gates, so acceptance can pass while operator observability remains incomplete.
